description: Commentary topics for Media Coach SimHub Plugin. Prompts are opinionated reactions to specific telemetry events.

topics.title: Spin / Big Save
topics.description: Extreme yaw rate — car nearly spun or driver caught a snap oversteer moment.

triggers.context: High yaw rate indicates the car is rotating well beyond driver intent

commentaryPrompts: That was a genuine save — the rear came fully around and somehow came back. The wheel just fought back against everything.
Nearly lost it right there. Full counter-steer at that speed is terrifying — you can feel the yaw rate through the entire car.
The rear stepped out hard on exit and that's the knife edge between a fast lap and a big shunt.
Caught that one but it took absolutely everything. One more degree of yaw and it's off into the barrier.
The car snapped and somehow the hands went the right way. That's pure instinct, not planning.

topics.title: Off Track
topics.description: Car went off-track, detected via large vertical acceleration spike.

triggers.context: Car left the track surface

commentaryPrompts: And that's a trip through the grass. Completely self-inflicted — brain said commit, feet said absolutely not.
Into the run-off. That's a tenth I'll never get back and probably several more besides.
Off track and the car just bounced through it. That's a combination of too much confidence and not enough car.
Went wide there and the gravel made the point better than I could. That corner keeps punishing me.
That's embarrassing. Absolutely embarrassing. Moving on.

topics.title: Hard Contact / Curb Impact
topics.description: Large vertical g-spike from hitting a curb hard, barrier tap, or contact.

triggers.context: Sudden vertical spike — curb, kerb, or contact

commentaryPrompts: Big hit. That's going to have done something to the car — you can feel it immediately through the wheel.
That was a proper impact and I felt it everywhere. Something just went wrong there.
Contact — and not the good kind. The car reacted and I just held on and hoped.
That kerb absolutely bit back. Took it too aggressively and the car launched.
Felt that one in my spine. That's not supposed to happen and yet here we are.

topics.title: ABS Firing
topics.description: ABS system activated — brakes locked before the system intervened.

triggers.context: ABS activated

commentaryPrompts: The ABS is working right now — you can feel it pulsing back through the pedal. Braked way too late for that approach.
ABS firing on that corner. The front is locking before the system catches it and I'm losing trail braking feel.
The brakes locked before the ABS intervened — either the tyres are past peak or I massively overshot the braking point.
ABS on. That's either impressive braking confidence or a complete misjudgement and I'm not sure which.
System took over under braking there. The threshold is right at the limit and I just went over it.

topics.title: Traction Control Cutting In
topics.description: Traction control activated on corner exit — rear sliding under power.

triggers.context: TC activated

commentaryPrompts: TC cutting in hard on exit — the rear wants to go and the computer is firmly saying no.
Traction control working overtime here. Too much throttle, too early, not enough tyre under the car.
That TC cut is costing me exit speed. Need to be smoother on the throttle or drop the setting.
The TC is on it — without intervention the wheelspin there would have been spectacular.
Traction control firing means I'm at the absolute edge of what this car can do on exit.

topics.title: Close Battle / Wheel-to-Wheel
topics.description: Another car is within close proximity on track.

triggers.context: Another car within 1.5% of track distance

commentaryPrompts: There's a car right on my gearbox and the pressure is immense. This is where you keep it absolutely clean.
Door to door at this speed — the margins are tiny and one mistake ends both our races.
Running wheel to wheel and this is exactly why sim racing is brilliant. Pure competition.
That close racing is intense — every braking point is a negotiation and the other driver knows it too.
The gap is nothing right now. I can see their braking references and they can see mine.

topics.title: Position Gained
topics.description: Player overtook another car and moved up in the running order.

triggers.context: Player moved up a position

commentaryPrompts: Got them! Clean move, fair fight, job done. Now it's about holding what we've just taken.
Past them! That's a position gained and it means everything for where we finish today.
Beautiful pass — read the corner, committed to it, made it stick. Exactly as planned.
Took that position and it felt absolutely right. The timing was perfect and the door was there.
That's how you do it — patient, clean, and decisive when the gap opened up.

topics.title: Position Lost
topics.description: Player was overtaken and dropped a position.

triggers.context: Player dropped a position

commentaryPrompts: And I've been passed. That's a position I'm not getting back without an actual fight.
They got me — went too wide and they were alongside before I could even react.
Lost that one and it stings. Clean pass on me — I have to give them credit but it hurts.
Position gone. That's on me — I gave them the opportunity and they took it immediately.
I've been passed and now I need to respond. Not spiralling, not panicking, just driving.

topics.title: Yellow Flag
topics.description: Yellow flag has gone out on track.

triggers.context: Yellow flag went active

commentaryPrompts: Yellow flag — something's happened ahead and the race just changed completely in those few seconds.
Caution out. Depending on the timing this either saves the strategy or destroys everything we've built.
Yellows are out and the field is bunching up. The restart is going to be critical.
Yellow flag and the race is paused. Use this time to reset, not to panic.
Caution — and now it's a waiting game. The safety car period is a lottery for strategy.

topics.title: Race Start
topics.description: Race session has started — lights out.

triggers.context: Race has begun

commentaryPrompts: Lights out! Everything on the line right now — stay clean through the first corner and race from there.
And we're racing! The start is everything — survive the first lap chaos and the rest is about execution.
Green flag! Months of laps in practice and it all comes down to the next ninety seconds of this opening lap.
Race is on. The launch was clean — now it's about the first corner and not throwing it away in the excitement.
Here we go! All that preparation, all that qualifying, and it's all about what happens in the next five seconds.

topics.title: Personal Best Lap
topics.description: Driver just set a new personal best lap time.

triggers.context: New personal best lap just completed

commentaryPrompts: That's a personal best! The car is working, the lap clicked, absolutely everything came together in that one.
PB! Every sector contributed to that — that's the best lap I've done at this circuit full stop.
New personal best and it felt like one from sector one. The lap was clean the entire way through.
That's a PB and the margin is real. The confidence is up and there's more time in the car if I can find it.
Personal best. The setup is right, the tyres are in the window, and the driving was clean. Perfect lap.

topics.title: Entering Pit Lane
topics.description: Player has entered the pit lane.

triggers.context: Player entered pit lane

commentaryPrompts: Into the pits and this stop needs to be clean — the race can be won or lost in pit lane at this point.
Pitting now. The call has been made and the strategy is committed. Let's see if it pays off.
Coming in for service and everything rests on the lap time we rejoin on and whether the gap is enough.
Pit entry and the tyres have absolutely given everything. Fresh rubber and we go again.
The pit stop window is open and we're in. This is the moment where the strategy either works or it doesn't.

topics.title: Maximum Cornering Load
topics.description: Car is pulling high lateral g-force — at the grip limit through a fast corner.

triggers.context: High lateral acceleration — near the grip limit in a fast corner

commentaryPrompts: That's serious lateral load through that corner — the grip level in this car is genuinely extraordinary.
Maximum cornering right there. The car just refuses to let go and the confidence that gives you is everything.
The G-forces through that corner are immense. You can feel every bit of it through the seat and the wheel.
The car is absolutely planted through there. The engineering that allows that at this speed is remarkable.
That's what this car was built for — maximum lateral load through a high-speed corner and it just eats it up.

topics.title: FFB Torque Spike
topics.description: Large sudden torque spike through the steering wheel — curb hit or snap load.

triggers.context: Sudden torque spike — curb hit, snap oversteer, or front loading up suddenly

commentaryPrompts: Massive kick through the wheel just then — hit something on the apex or caught a bump I absolutely wasn't ready for.
The wheel fought back hard right there. That torque spike tells me the front loaded up in a way the FFB is communicating perfectly.
Big FFB spike and you felt that one. The car just reacted to something I wasn't expecting and the wheel told me immediately.
That torque spike through the steering — either a kerb, a bump, or the front just stepped out and snapped back.
The force feedback is communicating everything that's happening at the contact patch. That spike was the car telling me something important.

topics.title: Low Fuel
topics.description: Fuel level is critically low — approaching the end of the stint.

triggers.context: Fuel at 15% or below — pit stop or fuel saving becoming critical

commentaryPrompts: Fuel is getting critical and the pressure is building. Every lap from here is a calculation.
Running low on fuel and the strategy has to be absolutely right. One miscalculation and we're walking.
The fuel light is on in my head right now. Depending on this stint I might be nursing it to the flag.
Low fuel means the car is lighter but the clock is ticking louder than ever. Very tight window.
Fuel critical — this is where experience matters. Lift, coast, manage consumption without losing the position.

topics.title: Wet Track Conditions
topics.description: Track conditions are declared wet or rain intensity is significant.

triggers.context: Track is wet

commentaryPrompts: The track is wet and everything has changed — completely different references, completely different feel through the wheel.
Wet conditions and the car is on edge in a way that no dry lap ever captures. Every input matters twice as much.
Rain changes everything. The grip level has dropped off a cliff and the braking points are completely different.
Wet track and the challenge is enormous. Reading the surface and finding the grip is everything right now.
The conditions are treacherous and I love it. This is where driving skill separates people more than anything else.

topics.title: High Tyre Wear
topics.description: Tyre wear has reached a high level — car behavior degrading.

triggers.context: Front left tyre at 65%+ wear
Front right tyre at 65%+ wear
Rear left tyre at 65%+ wear

commentaryPrompts: The tyres are going off and you can feel it in every corner — the front end is getting looser on entry.
Tyre wear is critical now. The rubber has given everything and the car is telling me in no uncertain terms.
Degradation is setting in hard. The laps are getting slower and there's genuinely nothing I can do about it.
The tyres have done their job but the job is almost done. Trying to manage what's left to the stop.
Worn tyres change the whole character of the car. Everything that was planted and predictable is now sliding and vague.

topics.title: Overheating Tyres
topics.description: Tyre temperatures have gone significantly over optimal operating range.

triggers.context: Front left tyre temperature critically high
Front right tyre temperature critically high

commentaryPrompts: Tyre temperatures are through the roof — past the peak operating window and into the overheating zone.
The tyres are overheating and the grip has simply gone. I'm sliding around and it's pure thermal degradation.
Temperatures too high and the rubber is greasing over. This is what kills a promising stint in real racing.
Hot tyres and I'm losing the car on every corner. Need to cool them down or just accept the consequences.
The thermal management battle is being lost right now. Too hot and the compound stops working entirely.

topics.title: Heavy Braking Zone
topics.description: Extreme longitudinal deceleration — very heavy braking event.

triggers.context: Very heavy deceleration — major braking zone

commentaryPrompts: That is a brutal braking zone — maximum deceleration from that speed is absolutely eye-watering.
The braking performance of this car is just insane. From that speed to nothing in an impossibly short distance.
Heavy braking and the car is dancing right on the limit. One mistake here and it's a spin or the barrier.
The G-forces under braking there are like nothing else on the lap. The nose just dips and it stops.
That's a big one — the carbon brakes biting and the whole car pitching forward under that deceleration.

topics.title: Qualifying Hot Lap
topics.description: Running significantly ahead of personal best in qualifying — hot lap in progress.

triggers.context: Running 0.4s ahead of personal best — very fast lap in progress

commentaryPrompts: This lap is coming together — every sector is cleaner than anything I've done today. Don't overthink it.
Hot lap in qualifying and the car feels absolutely on rails. Stay calm, nail the last sector.
The delta is positive across the whole lap. This could be a new pole time if I don't mess up the last chicane.
Lap of my life potentially coming up. The conditions are perfect, the car is dialled in, just execute.
Every corner so far has been right on the money. This is the lap — don't brake early, don't run wide.

topics.title: iRacing Incident Points
topics.description: iRacing incident count increased — contact or track limits violation registered.

triggers.context: iRacing incident points just increased

commentaryPrompts: That's incident points on the iRacing record. The system saw that and I completely deserved it.
Incident count goes up. iRacing doesn't forget anything and that contact just cost me Safety Rating.
Another incident point — the car and the stewards both saw it. My SR is going to take a proper hit.
Incidents are mounting and the iRating consequences are real. I need to drive significantly cleaner.
That's been flagged. In iRacing you can't hide from your mistakes and that one is now on my record.

topics.title: ERS Battery Low
topics.description: ERS battery is nearly depleted — losing hybrid power advantage.

triggers.context: ERS battery at 15% or below

commentaryPrompts: ERS is almost depleted — no more free speed until the battery regenerates through the next braking zone.
The hybrid is empty. From here to the next regen zone I'm running on pure internal combustion.
Battery gone and the performance delta is immediately noticeable — like losing a full gear of acceleration.
ERS depleted and now every straight is a negotiation. The car feels completely different without the electric motor.
The hybrid system is the difference between competitive and not. Without it right now I'm vulnerable.

topics.title: High Track Temperature
topics.description: Track temperature is very high — affects tyre operating range and degradation.

triggers.context: Track temperature is very high — tyres will reach peak temp faster

commentaryPrompts: Track temperature is baking out there. The rubber goes down fast in these conditions and the grip comes and goes.
Hot track means the tyres peak early and fall off hard. It's a brutal tyre management exercise.
The tarmac is forty-something degrees — you can feel the compound struggling to maintain consistent grip.
Track temps like this means thermal management becomes the whole race. Pace without preservation is pointless.
In this heat the track surface is slippery in a very particular way — not cold, just over the limit of what the tyre wants.

topics.title: DRS Open
topics.description: DRS rear wing is open and active — experiencing the straight-line speed boost.

triggers.context: DRS is active and the rear wing is open

commentaryPrompts: DRS is open and the straight-line advantage is genuinely real — you feel the drag drop away and the car just surges.
Rear wing open and the top speed delta is significant. Whether that's enough to make the pass is the question.
DRS zone and I'm flat out. The aerodynamic difference is immediately obvious at this speed.
The rear wing is open and this is the decision point — commit to the outside line or go defensive.
DRS active and the car leaps forward in a way that completely changes the overtaking calculation.

topics.title: Car Balance — Sustained Load
topics.description: Car is carrying high lateral and yaw load simultaneously — well-balanced through a complex corner.

triggers.context: High lateral load with significant yaw — car rotating well through a corner

commentaryPrompts: The balance of this car right now is something else — rotating exactly where you want it, completely on command.
Perfect oversteer on demand and the front is doing precisely what I'm asking. This is the car working with you.
The car is talking to me through this section — completely planted, completely communicative, completely alive.
When the setup is right you feel it in a corner like that. Absolutely hooked up and entirely in control.
That's what a well-sorted car feels like — load it up, rotate it, and it does exactly what you asked.
